description:
This ecological zonation diagram illustrates the distribution of land cover types across an altitudinal gradient in a mountain environment. From highest altitude (left) to lowest altitude (right):
1.  The uppermost section of the slope is designated as "Bare rocks".
2.  Immediately below the bare rocks, the terrain transitions to "Grasses with bare soil".
3.  Further down the slope, extensive areas of "Alpine meadows" are depicted.
4.  A horizontal dashed line, labelled "Tree line", marks the boundary between the alpine zone and lower elevations.
5.  Below the tree line, the landscape is characterised by "Mountain hay meadows".
6.  At the lowest visible altitude, at the foot of the mountain slope, human settlements (houses) are shown.
